message:
test(controller): drop redundant outer guard; always scan prompt line-by-line

Fresh-eyes pass on 048d0b5d spotted a theoretical regression-escape in
TestResolveControllerPrompt_DefaultTemplate: the outer
`strings.Contains(content, "ntm send myproject --panes") && ...`
short-circuit was meant as a fast-path, but it short-circuits past the
per-line scan in exactly the scenarios where the scan would catch a
regression. Concrete failure: a future edit that removes the multi-pane
example line but keeps (or reintroduces) `ntm send myproject --pane N
--msg=...` would render the outer condition false (no `--panes` plural
anywhere in the content) and skip the inner loop entirely, passing the
test with the bug present.

Drop the outer guard and always scan line-by-line — it's a handful of
string compares on a ~30-line template, not a hot path — matching the
shape already used in TestDefaultControllerPromptContent. Behavior on
the current (healthy) template is unchanged; the difference shows up
only under a regression that the test was supposed to catch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
